judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with strong repository citations; they are comparable in quality and completeness.

report_1.evidence: Per `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`, failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap

report_2.evidence: Per `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`, checkout failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap.

report_1.evidence: records that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded

report_2.evidence: The internal timeline `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` sets a contractual monthly SLA of 99.9%, with any month below that being a breach that owes service credits scaled to the depth of the breach. April 2026 was 99.77% (`larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), so this incident caused a breach.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` sets a contractual monthly uptime SLA of 99.9%, with any month below it a breach owing service credits that scale with the depth of the breach. `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 at 99.77% — a breach.

report_1.evidence: violates rule 1 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` ("Never assign blame to an individual")

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual."

report_1.evidence: Rule 3 of the style guide requires leading with customer impact and remediation; the draft instead opens with an uptime boast (lines 3–4), buries the (understated) impact mid-document, and puts the single remediation in the final line.

report_2.evidence: The draft must be restructured to lead with customer impact and remediation.** Rule 3 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md`. The draft instead opens with a (false) uptime boast ... the sole remediation item is one sentence at the end (line 21).
